entity_relationships.business_meaning: Each department contains multiple employees; one-to-many relationship (a department has many employees, each employee belongs to one department).

Employees are assigned to shifts via schedules, have one or more roles via employee_role, may be assigned equipment, and belong to a department; many-to-many with shifts and roles, one-to-many with department.
Shifts are populated by employees through schedules; many-to-many relationship via the schedule table.
Roles are assigned to employees through employee_role; many-to-many relationship (an employee can have multiple roles, a role can be held by many employees).

A flight has baggage items, is assigned a gate via gate_assignment, a stand via stand_assignment, and undergoes one or more turnaround operations; one-to-many with baggage and turnaround, one-to-one with gate and stand per assignment.
Gates are assigned to flights via gate_assignment; many-to-many relationship (a gate hosts many flights over time, a flight uses one gate).
Stands are assigned to flights via stand_assignment; many-to-many relationship (a stand accommodates many flights, a flight uses one stand).
Equipment belongs to a type, is assigned to employees via equipment_assignment, and has maintenance requests and history; many-to-one with equipment_type, many-to-many with employees.
Each baggage item belongs to a flight and has scans, loading, unloading, and may be flagged as lost; one-to-many with related baggage tables.
Each turnaround belongs to a flight and consists of multiple tasks (cleaning, refueling, catering, boarding) and checklist items assigned to employees; one-to-many with each task type.

business_processes.tables: department, employee, shift, schedule, attendance, role, employee_role, leave_request, training_record
gate, stand, flight, turnaround_operation, cleaning_task, refueling_task, catering_task, boarding_task, turnaround_checklist, gate_assignment, stand_assignment

use_cases.name: Employee Shift Scheduling
use_cases.description: Manage employee work schedules by assigning them to shifts across days, tracking attendance and leave requests.

use_cases.name: Flight Turnaround Management
use_cases.description: Coordinate all ground operations for a flight turnaround including cleaning, refueling, catering, and boarding tasks with assigned employees.
use_cases.involved_tables: flight, turnaround_operation, cleaning_task, refueling_task, catering_task, boarding_task, turnaround_checklist, employee

use_cases.name: Equipment Maintenance Tracking
use_cases.description: Track equipment assignments to employees, log maintenance requests, and maintain maintenance history for operational assets.
use_cases.involved_tables: equipment, equipment_type, equipment_assignment, maintenance_request, maintenance_history, employee

use_cases.name: Baggage Lifecycle Management
use_cases.description: Track baggage items from creation through scans, loading, unloading, and resolve lost baggage incidents.
use_cases.involved_tables: baggage, flight, baggage_scan, baggage_loading, baggage_unloading, lost_baggage

use_cases.name: Gate and Stand Allocation
use_cases.description: Assign available gates and stands to arriving and departing flights while avoiding conflicts.

sample_queries.question: List all employees scheduled for a specific shift on a given date along with their department.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, d.department_name, s.shift_start, s.shift_end
FROM employee e
JOIN schedule sc ON e.employee_id = sc.employee_id
JOIN shift s ON sc.shift_id = s.shift_id
JOIN department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE s.shift_date = '2024-01-15' AND s.shift_type = 'Morning';

sample_queries.question: Get all turnaround operations for a specific flight with task completion status.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT to_.turnaround_id, f.flight_number, ct.task_status AS cleaning, rt.task_status AS refueling, cat.task_status AS catering, bt.task_status AS boarding
FROM turnaround_operation to_
JOIN flight f ON to_.flight_id = f.flight_id
LEFT JOIN cleaning_task ct ON to_.turnaround_id = ct.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN refueling_task rt ON to_.turnaround_id = rt.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN catering_task cat ON to_.turnaround_id = cat.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN boarding_task bt ON to_.turnaround_id = bt.turnaround_id
WHERE f.flight_number = 'AA1234';

sample_queries.question: Find all equipment with open maintenance requests grouped by equipment type.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT et.type_name, COUNT(mr.request_id) AS open_requests
FROM equipment e
JOIN equipment_type et ON e.equipment_type_id = et.equipment_type_id
JOIN maintenance_request mr ON e.equipment_id = mr.equipment_id
WHERE mr.status = 'Open'
GROUP BY et.type_name
ORDER BY open_requests DESC;

sample_queries.question: Track a specific baggage tag through all scan events from check-in to final delivery.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT b.baggage_tag, bs.scan_time, bs.scan_location, bs.scan_type
FROM baggage b
JOIN baggage_scan bs ON b.baggage_id = bs.baggage_id
WHERE b.baggage_tag = 'BAG987654'
ORDER BY bs.scan_time;

sample_queries.question: Identify lost baggage items along with their associated flight and last known scan location.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT lb.report_id, b.baggage_tag, f.flight_number, bs.scan_location, lb.report_date, lb.status
FROM lost_baggage lb
JOIN baggage b ON lb.baggage_id = b.baggage_id
JOIN flight f ON b.flight_id = f.flight_id
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (SELECT scan_location FROM baggage_scan WHERE baggage_id = b.baggage_id ORDER BY scan_time DESC LIMIT 1) bs ON TRUE
WHERE lb.status = 'Open';
